message.content:
## minimist — Suggested Knowledge-Base Updates

### Current Status

```markdown
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Latest version** | 1.2.8 |
| **Repository** | [github.com/minimistjs/minimist](https://github.com/minimistjs/minimist) |
| **Weekly npm downloads** | ~118.2 million |
| **Maintenance status** | Low-activity; last release (1.2.8) published in 2023. PR [#24](https://github.com/minimistjs/minimist/pull/24) (robustness rework of `isConstructorOrProto`) was merged 2023-02-25. |
```

### Vulnerability Table

```markdown
| CVE | GHSA | Severity | Summary | Affected versions | Patched versions | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [CVE-2020-7598](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2020-7598) | [GHSA-vh95-rmgr-6w4m](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vh95-rmgr-6w4m) | Medium | Prototype Pollution — unsanitized arguments allow modification of `Object.prototype` (e.g., `--__proto__.y=Polluted`). Exploitable when attackers control CLI arguments passed to `minimist`. | `< 0.2.1` · `>= 1.0.0, < 1.2.3` | 0.2.1 / 1.2.3 | Published 2020-04-03. Multiple fix commits across both release lines. |
| [CVE-2021-44906](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-44906) | [GHSA-xvch-5gv4-984h](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xvch-5gv4-984h) | **Critical** | Prototype Pollution in `setKey()` (`index.js`, lines 69–95). Separate vector from CVE-2020-7598. | `< 0.2.4` · `>= 1.0.0, < 1.2.6` | 0.2.4 / 1.2.6 | Published 2022-03-18. Back-port to 0.2.x was reworked in [PR #24](https://github.com/minimistjs/minimist/pull/24) (merged 2023-02-25) to align `isConstructorOrProto` with the mainline implementation. |
```

### Security Posture Notes

```markdown
- **Both known prototype-pollution vulnerabilities are resolved** in the current
  latest release (1.2.8) and in 0.2.4+ on the legacy line.
- No additional GHSA advisories are currently published against minimist beyond
  the two listed above.
- The 0.2.x back-port of the CVE-2021-44906 fix was initially flawed; PR #24
  corrected `isConstructorOrProto` to match mainline logic. Consumers still on
  0.2.x should verify they are on ≥ 0.2.4 **and** include the PR #24 fix
  (included in 0.2.4 as released on npm).
- Prototype-pollution exploitability requires that attacker-controlled strings
  are passed as arguments to `minimist`. CLI tools that only parse trusted
  process arguments have a reduced attack surface, but library consumers
  accepting untrusted input should treat this as a real risk.
```

### Open Questions

```markdown
- [ ] Is `minimist` still actively maintained? The repository shows minimal
      commit activity since early 2023. Projects with strict maintenance
      requirements may want to evaluate alternatives (e.g., `yargs-parser`,
      `mri`).
- [ ] Are there any unreported bypass variants of the `isConstructorOrProto`
      guard in 1.2.8? No public evidence exists, but no formal audit has been
      disclosed either.
```

---

## semver — Suggested Knowledge-Base Updates

### Current Status

```markdown
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Latest version** | 7.7.4 |
| **Repository** | [github.com/npm/node-semver](https://github.com/npm/node-semver) |
| **Weekly npm downloads** | ~632.7 million |
| **Maintenance status** | Actively maintained by the npm CLI team; regular releases continue on the 7.x line. |
```

### Vulnerability Table

```markdown
| CVE | GHSA | Severity | Summary | Affected versions | Patched versions | Fix details |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [CVE-2015-8855](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2015-8855
